title: Free Outdoor Yoga with MoreYoga at Wembley Park
address: Elvin Gardens, Wembley Park, London HA9
description: Gentle outdoor Vinyasa flow session led by MoreYoga instructor Ingrid Falcao, open to all levels with mats provided first come first served. Runs every Friday morning until 7 August 2026; free but booking via Eventbrite required.

title: Life Drawing After Work (4-week course)
address: Keeley Street, Covent Garden, London WC2B 4BA
description: Relaxed Friday-evening life drawing course with tutor Joe Richardson, drawing a variety of life models in different materials and poses — no experience needed. Four weekly sessions starting 10 July.

title: Simply Being 5Rhythms (South Bank)
description: Richard Wiltshire's weekly Friday 5Rhythms class on the South Bank; next class 10 July (no class 31 July or 14 August).

title: Thursday: Singles Badminton in Haggerston
description: Singles badminton night by the Thursday dating app, a short walk from Bethnal Green. Repeats on 24 July.

title: Circling Drop-In Evening (with Robyn Wilford)
venue: Community venue opposite Elephant & Castle station
description: A drop-in evening of Circling, a present-moment relational awareness practice bringing curiosity to what unfolds within yourself, between people and in the group. Recurring drop-in; newcomers welcome.

title: Lifepath99 + Speedial + SPRING at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, Hackney, E9 6NU
description: Cheap Friday-night triple bill of new bands under the glitter ceiling of Hackney's ex-servicemen's club turned venue. Doors 7pm.

title: Uyen Luu Vietnamese Supper Club
description: Long-running homemade Vietnamese supper club by cookbook author Uyen Luu — a multi-course feast at one shared long table in her canal-side Hackney studio. Also runs 11, 17 and 18 July; book via the form on the page.

title: Campfire Club: Rachel Sermanni + Ceitidh Mac
description: Outdoor summer folk concert around the campfire at Cody Dock on the River Lea, with dreamy Scottish indie-folk from Rachel Sermanni and cello-and-vocals from Ceitidh Mac.

title: Poetry International: Benjamin Zephaniah Celebration
description: Opening night of the Poetry International Festival (10–12 Jul): an evening of poetry, music and memories celebrating Benjamin Zephaniah, with Lemn Sissay, Pauline Black, Jackie Kay and Michael Rosen. Exact start time unconfirmed; evening event.

title: Cheza Roho: Dance Meditation Games
description: Mikel The Energy's signature Rhythm & Breath experience combining movement, breath, rhythm and music through interactive dance meditation games. Runs weekly on Friday evenings.

title: Pigeon Dog + Top Shagger + Lots Of at Paper Dress Vintage
description: Friday-night triple bill of raw grunge, alternative noise and post-rock — same scrappy east London pub-gig energy as the Shacklewell Arms circuit.

title: Angel Comedy at The Bill Murray
address: 39 Queen's Head Street, Islington, London N1 8NQ
description: London's community comedy club runs shows seven nights a week, including free pay-what-you-think pro nights on Fridays and Saturdays with TV-credited acts. Start time approximate — shows run from late afternoon to 11pm, so check the listings; arrive early for free shows.

title: The Sauna Rave (Prime Time x Flow State)
description: Friday-night rave inside the Sanctuary's custom-built sauna, mixing heat, music and recovery; this edition is hosted with Flow State and Prime Time. Recurs weekly on Fridays.

title: Alexander Technique: One-Day Workshop (City Lit)
description: A one-day beginner's introduction to the Alexander Technique covering everyday movement patterns, spatial awareness and practical ways to reduce tension and improve posture and breathing. Wear loose clothing for floor work.

title: Ecstatic Dance Gathering Nr.Two
description: Outdoor ecstatic dance in the Hackney Wick woods with live DJs, guided embodiment practices and a sacred fire gathering to close.

title: Lewis Capaldi at BST Hyde Park
description: Lewis Capaldi headlines a major open-air festival day in Hyde Park with special guests Conan Gray, Jacob Alon, The Vaccines and Absolutely; he plays a second show on 12 July. Gates time unconfirmed; afternoon start typical, tickets still available.

title: Rhythm Roots + Embodied Expression
description: A dance class for everyone, exploring a wide range of movement styles and embodied expression with Lyndon The Healing Tree. Recurs weekly on Saturday evenings.

title: The Sunset Sessions — open-air ecstatic dance (Saturday edition)
venue: Secret park location, Regent's Park area
description: Ecstatic Dance London's outdoor summer session: silent-disco headphone ecstatic dance under the open sky with cacao circle and picnic social — no booze, no shoes, no phones. Repeats 15, 17 and 18 July.

title: Sparks & Steps: Summer Singles Event (ages 28-40)
description: Summer singles social in the City with low-pressure ice-breakers, conversation prompts and DJs playing pop and house. Aimed at ages 28-40.

title: Statistics and Stupidly Smart AI (Royal Institution talk)
description: Saturday-evening Royal Institution lecture on the statistics underpinning today's startlingly capable AI systems — a science-talk fit for the AI-curious.

title: Columbia Road Flower Market
description: East London's famous street flower market, held every Sunday from 8am until around 3pm, with plants and cut flowers plus 60 independent shops, cafes and galleries along the Victorian street.

title: Wheel Throwing Pottery Taster Class
description: A three-hour hands-on introduction to throwing clay on the pottery wheel for complete beginners, with one piece trimmed, glazed and fired for you to collect. Max nine people; further dates on 2 and 23 August.

title: Sound & Silence Festival (alcohol-free, donation-based)
venue: Multiple venues across Hackney Wick (check-in at Bar90)
description: A 12-hour sober journey through Hackney Wick: ecstatic dance, breathwork, sound baths, gong sessions, singing circles, yoga, live music, comedy and wellness talks. Many sessions need free advance booking due to capacity.

title: Bottle Factory Flea at Hackney Bridge
description: South London's Bottle Factory Flea crosses the river for its biggest market yet: 50-60 traders selling vintage clothing, furniture, records and homeware, plus street food by the canal.

title: Thursday: Singles Museum Walk and Lunch
description: Singles museum walk around the National Portrait Gallery followed by lunch, run by the Thursday dating app.

title: Summer Sundays at Victoria Park Bandstand: London City Brass + Metropolitan Brass
description: Free outdoor bandstand concert from the Vicky Park Friends Group — bring a blanket and picnic.

title: London Rationalish Meetup (LessWrong/ACX community)
address: Lincoln's Inn Fields, Holborn, London WC2A
description: Casual outdoor gathering of London's rationalist/ACX crowd; organisers arrive from 2pm and discussion of three readings starts around 3pm. Weather permitting, in the park's northeast quadrant.

title: Jam Session at Newspeak House
description: Open collaborative jam in Newspeak Hall — bring an instrument or your voice, or just come to listen.

title: Boy Blue In The Park
description: One-night hip hop dance theatre celebration from award-winning company Boy Blue, featuring their adult companies on the open-air stage. Performance time unconfirmed; evening show expected.

title: Sunday Jazz at The Old Blue Last
description: Free weekly jazz session every Sunday evening in the upstairs room of Shoreditch's legendary pub venue. Repeats weekly through August.

title: Monday Contact Improv Jam: Dress-Up Jam
description: Weekly Monday CI jam hosted by Marie Chabert (22 years of CI practice): facilitated warm-up then open jam, this week with a playful dress-up theme. First-timers can arrive 6:20pm for a consent/guidelines intro. Jams pause after 20 July, resuming 7 September.

title: Campaign Lab Hack Night
description: Working session for progressive technologists and activists building election tools. Recurs roughly fortnightly, with further dates on 27 July and 3 August.

title: Krishna Das — Home in the Heart (kirtan)
description: Legendary kirtan/chant artist Krishna Das leads an evening of call-and-response mantra singing at Union Chapel — a strong match for the ecstatic dance and embodiment scene. Doors 18:30; a second night runs 14 July.

title: Men's Circle: Accountability
description: Facilitated men's circle exploring personal accountability and self-trust through meditation, embodiment, sharing and reflection. Small group (under 10).

title: CLR Summer Fellowship Speaker Series: Daniel Kokotajlo (AI Futures Project)
venue: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), 25 Holywell Row, London EC2A 4XE, UK
description:

title: Grounding Practice: Disruption and Innovation with Metal & Water
description: Informal salon talk with Nancy May Roberts, founder of performance company Metal & Water, on sustaining a creative practice: commissioning, funding structures and navigating institutions.

title: Alive: What Life Is, How It Works, and Why It Matters (Royal Institution talk)
description: An evening science talk in the Royal Institution's historic lecture theatre exploring what life actually is and how it works. In-person theatre tickets from £7.90; also streamed online.

title: Dancing Awareness — Live Music Edition
description: Meditation and free movement combined, with live percussion, clarinet and banjo. Group awareness practice followed by spontaneous dance; open to all experience levels.

title: Somerset House Summer Series 2026 (opening night: Naïka)
venue: Somerset House, Edmond J. Safra Fountain Court
description: Eleven nights of live music (16–26 Jul) in Somerset House's courtyard, opening with French-Haitian pop/R&B artist Naïka. Several nights including Naïka, Palace (17 Jul) and Thee Sacred Souls (18 Jul) are sold out; check other dates.

title: Lydia Lunch & Marc Hurtado play SUICIDE and Alan Vega at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, Hackney, E9 6NU
description: No-wave icon Lydia Lunch and Marc Hurtado perform the music of Suicide and Alan Vega in a rare small-room London show. Doors 7:30pm.

title: CLR Summer Fellowship Speaker Series: Lewis Hammond (Cooperative AI Foundation)
venue: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), 25 Holywell Row, London EC2A 4XE, UK

title: London Craft Beer Festival 2026
description: Two-day beer festival (17–18 Jul) with over 800 craft beers from 100+ breweries, street food and live music; all-inclusive tickets cover unlimited sampling. Friday evening session listed; Saturday has day and evening sessions.

title: First Night of the Proms 2026
description: The BBC Proms season opens with Dalia Stasevska conducting the BBC Symphony Orchestra and Chorus: Copland's Fanfare for the Common Man, Gershwin's An American in Paris, and pianist Yunchan Lim playing Ravel's Piano Concerto in G.

title: Forage and Meditate on London Canals
description: Guided foraging walk along the canal towards Hackney Marshes, blending seasonal wild-food identification and tastings with practical mindfulness, ending in a grounding meditation.

title: Sanctum — movement, breathwork & somatic release
description: An immersive, music-driven mind-body practice blending dynamic movement with breathwork, meditation and somatic release. Recurs on weekend mornings.

title: Sicilian Cooking Class at Food at 52
description: A 3.5-hour hands-on Sicilian cookery class at Clerkenwell's well-loved Food at 52 school, where you cook and then eat a full Sicilian spread. Part of a rolling schedule of world-cuisine classes through July and August.

title: Grow's 12th Birthday — all-day canalside live music party
address: Main Yard, Wallis Road, Hackney Wick, E9 5LN
description: Canalside venue Grow celebrates its 12th birthday with a full day and night of live music, DJs and food by the water; the free daytime stretch is the sweet spot before it shifts to a late-night party.

title: CI Goldsmiths: Contact Improv Class + Jam with Live Soundscape
description: A 2-hour contact improvisation class followed by a 3-hour open jam with live soundscape, run by the long-standing CI Goldsmiths collective with a different teacher each session. A deep-dive Saturday format ideal for building CI skills.

title: Afrique Express! at Servant Jazz Quarters
description: Free late-night African-influenced live session in SJQ's tiny basement bar off Gillett Square. Starts 9pm.

title: Monday Contact Improv: Class + Jam
description: Marie Chabert teaches a thematic CI class with somatic guided exploration, partner work and technique, followed by a 45-60 minute open jam. Explicitly welcomes beginners through to seasoned professionals; doors 6:20pm.

description:

title: Thursday: Singles Pottery Night
description: Singles pottery-painting night by the Thursday dating app — dating plus a hands-on craft in one evening.

title: Thought Experiments in Pubs: Living with Tech
description: Small-group discussion evening exploring technology and AI through thought experiments and rotating conversations.

title: Big Dork Summer: Coach Party at Sebright Arms
description: Isle of Wight indie-rockers Coach Party play an up-close basement show as part of the Big Dork Summer series at this Bethnal Green pub venue.

title: Regent's Park Outdoor CI Jam
description: Free outdoor summer contact improvisation jam with rotating facilitators: social time from 6pm, opening circle 6:40pm, jamming until 8:45pm. Runs Wednesday evenings weekly through 19 August; check the listing for the exact meeting spot.

title: Cowgirls & Egyptians: Blue Blow and Witchy Brews (Darren Le Baron)
description: Evening talk tracing Goddess wisdom, entheogenic sacraments and Egyptian mystery traditions through African cosmology, from the teacher behind the Shroomshop workshops.

title: Thursday: 30+ Singles Night — new venue, Shoreditch
description: 30+ singles night by the Thursday dating app at a just-opened Shoreditch venue.

title: Shroomshop: Mushroom Cultivation Workshop
description: Hands-on evening workshop covering grain spawn, fruiting tubs and aftercare for growing gourmet mushrooms at home; everyone leaves with their own grow kit.

title: Thursday: Singles Night — new venue, Shoreditch
description: Singles night by the Thursday dating app at a just-opened Shoreditch venue.

title: Dancing Freedom in the Woods
venue: Woodland, Hackney Wick (exact spot shared on booking)
description: Substance-free ecstatic dance in a Hackney Wick woodland with a DJ-led wave through expansive musical genres, closing with a fireside gathering for integration and connection.

title: HYPER JAPAN Festival 2026
description: The UK's largest Japanese culture festival runs 24–26 July with over 530 exhibitors covering music, food, fashion, gaming, workshops and shopping. Friday hours 12–8pm; children 10 and under free.

title: MATS X LISA X LASR SUMMER SOCIAL
venue: London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA), 25 Holywell Row, London EC2A 4XE, UK

title: True Dating: Speed Dating in Bank (ages 30-45)
description: Friday-night mixed speed dating for ages 30-45 at The Wren Tavern by St Paul's; male tickets nearly gone at time of checking.

title: Campfire Club: Junior Brother + Anna McLuckie
description: Friday-evening outdoor Campfire Club session at Cody Dock with Irish alt-folk songwriter Junior Brother and harpist-singer Anna McLuckie. Price not yet listed (the 10 July edition is £20).

title: Discourse: Discoveries at the Icy Moons of Jupiter and Saturn
description: A free evening Discourse lecture at the Royal Institution on the latest discoveries at the icy moons of Jupiter and Saturn, in the famous Faraday lecture theatre.

title: CGI Goth Wife: EP Release Show at The Victoria Dalston
description: EP release party in the back room of this Victorian pub venue on Kingsland Road. Doors 7:30pm, with a free indie club night following.

title: Terrarium Making Workshop with London Terrariums
description: A 90-minute hands-on workshop building your own glass terrarium to take home, run by specialists London Terrariums. Further dates through late July and August, including evening sessions on 29 July and 13 August.

title: PsySoc Community Walk, Speed-Friending & Nature Connection
venue: Hampstead Heath (meeting point emailed after booking)
description: Psychedelic Society guided heath walk mixing mindfulness, meditation and speed-friending — open to all, solo attendance encouraged.

title: Campaign Lab Summer BBQ 2026
description: Casual all-day summer gathering for the Campaign Lab community with food, drinks and networking.

title: A Bohm Dialogue on Love
description: Group inquiry using David Bohm's dialogue method to explore love and connection through deep listening in a non-hierarchical circle — close kin to circling and authentic relating practice.

title: CATS at Regent's Park Open Air Theatre (opening night)
description: A brand-new open-air production of Andrew Lloyd Webber's musical based on T.S. Eliot's Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats, running 25 July – 19 September. Evening performance time approximate.

title: Summer Sundays at Victoria Park Bandstand: Hackney Community Orchestra + Disco Lizards
description: Free Sunday bandstand session with the Hackney Community Orchestra and Disco Lizards — picnic-blanket format in the park.

title: Resistance Ecstatic Dance & Yoga
description: Sunday evening freeform ecstatic dance journey at a Greenwich garden plot with opening circle, guided warmup and a wind-down around the bonfire with tea and snacks.

title: MOTION LONDON — 5Rhythms Movement Gathering
venue: Goldsmiths Great Hall, Richard Hoggart Building
description: Large-scale movement gathering dancing through the 5Rhythms map with live DJ on a big wooden dance floor — 'nothing to learn and everything to remember'. No phones, no spectating. Also runs Mon 24 Aug.

title: Mini Golf Singles Party (ages 28-42) — True Dating
description: Speed-dating alternative where singles aged 28-42 mingle over rounds of tech-enhanced mini golf at Puttshack in the City. Tickets still available for both men and women at time of checking.

title: Simply Being 5Rhythms Full Moon Garden Session
description: Outdoor full-moon 5Rhythms garden session with Richard Wiltshire in Brockwell Park.

title: Heart Circle — pause, reconnect & explore
description: A slow, held sharing circle using meditation, somatic practice and shared inquiry to deepen connection with self, community and nature — the closest thing to authentic relating in London this window.

title: West London Supper Club – Summer Edition
description: An intimate 12-seat supper club with a curated multi-course Persian menu and welcome drink, designed for conversation and meeting new people in a cosy Kensington cafe-gallery.

title: Big Scary Indian (NYC) + Mr Speed + Teleboutique at The Shacklewell Arms
description: New York's Big Scary Indian heads a cheap three-band Friday bill in the Shacklewell's back room. Doors 8pm.

title: Thursday: Sauna Rave at Sanctuary &Soul
description: Singles edition of the Shoreditch sauna rave, run with the Thursday dating app at Sanctuary &Soul.

title: Crash Of Rhinos at MOTH Club
address: Old Trades Hall, Valette Street, Hackney, E9 6NU
description: Cult Derby emo/post-hardcore band Crash Of Rhinos play a reunion show at Hackney's MOTH Club. Doors 7pm.

title: Connection: Our Deepest Desire and Greatest Fear — Circling Day Workshop
description: A day-long facilitated Circling workshop exploring the tension between our yearning for connection and the fear that comes with it. One listing showed 9 Aug — double-check the date when booking.

title: Land and Belonging: Josh Schrei and Alexander Beiner in Conversation
description: The Emerald podcast's Josh Schrei and sensemaking writer Alexander Beiner explore immigration, land and belonging through a mythic lens, hosted by the Psychedelic Society.

title: Slow Mass at The Victoria Dalston
description: Chicago post-hardcore/indie outfit Slow Mass bring their intricate, heavy melodies to the Victoria's small back-room stage. Doors 7pm.

title: Ebony — live at Village Underground
description: Evening gig by Ebony at the converted Victorian warehouse venue in Shoreditch, a short walk from Bethnal Green.

title: The Next Chapter of Psychedelics with Robin Carhart-Harris
description: Neuroscientist Robin Carhart-Harris discusses where psychedelic science goes next, including his REBUS model and forthcoming book, followed by social mingling.

title: Simply Being 5Rhythms New Moon Eclipse Garden Special
description: Outdoor new-moon eclipse 5Rhythms garden special with Richard Wiltshire in Brockwell Park.

title: closeup summer series: Eaves Wilder at Sebright Arms
description: Rising London indie-pop songwriter Eaves Wilder plays a stripped-back basement show as part of closeup's 2026 summer series.

title: Alexander Technique: An Introduction (City Academy)
description: A 3-hour evening introduction to the Alexander Technique with teacher Cristina Codina Llavina, focused on improving posture and releasing habitual tension. Open to complete beginners.
